Class Graph

java.lang.Object
io.graphite.graph.Graph
All Implemented Interfaces:
IGraph
Direct Known Subclasses:
DirectedGraph, UndirectedGraph
public abstract class Graph extends Object implements IGraph
Default mutable implementation of IGraph.

Graph provides the core implementation used throughout the Graphite framework. It stores the graph structure, manages graph metadata, and acts as the gateway to all algorithm services.

Rather than exposing algorithms directly, this class follows a service-oriented architecture. Each algorithm category is accessed through a dedicated service that is created lazily and cached for reuse.

IGraph graph = Graphs.undirected()
        .addEdge(0, 1)
        .addEdge(1, 2)
        .build();

graph.traversal().bfs(0);
graph.shortestPath().dijkstra(0);
graph.mst().prim();

Features

  • Mutable graph implementation.
  • Adjacency-list based storage.
  • Lazy initialization of algorithm services.
  • Cached service instances.
  • Supports graph transformation and formatting.

Service Delegation

Algorithm execution is delegated to specialized service objects such as Traversal, ShortestPath, and MST. Services are created on first access and reused for the lifetime of the graph, minimizing object allocation while keeping the public API clean.

Thread Safety

This implementation is mutable and therefore not inherently thread-safe. Concurrent modifications should be externally synchronized.

For read-only usage, consider converting the graph to an immutable instance using asImmutable().

Since:
2.0
See Also:
  • IGraph
  • ImmutableGraph
  • Graphs

    • getNeighbors

      public List<Edge> getNeighbors(int vertex)
      Description copied from interface: IGraph
      Returns the neighboring vertices adjacent to the specified vertex.

      For directed graphs, this method returns the outgoing neighbors of the supplied vertex.

      Specified by:
      getNeighbors in interface IGraph
      Parameters:
      vertex - the source vertex
      Returns:
      the neighboring vertices

    • getEdges

      public List<Edge> getEdges()
      Description copied from interface: IGraph
      Returns all edges contained in this graph.

      The returned collection represents the current edge set of the graph. The iteration order is implementation dependent.

      Specified by:
      getEdges in interface IGraph
      Returns:
      an immutable view or snapshot of the graph edges

    • getVertices

      public int getVertices()
      Description copied from interface: IGraph
      Returns the total number of vertices contained in this graph.

      Vertices are identified by zero-based integer indices ranging from 0 to getVertices() - 1.

      Specified by:
      getVertices in interface IGraph
      Returns:
      the number of vertices in this graph

    • edgeCount

      public int edgeCount()
      Specified by:
      edgeCount in interface IGraph

    • isWeighted

      public boolean isWeighted()
      Description copied from interface: IGraph
      Returns whether this graph contains weighted edges.
      Specified by:
      isWeighted in interface IGraph
      Returns:
      true if edge weights are significant; false otherwise

    • asImmutable

      public IGraph asImmutable()
      Description copied from interface: IGraph
      Returns an immutable view of this graph.

      If this graph is already immutable, implementations may simply return the current instance.

      Specified by:
      asImmutable in interface IGraph
      Returns:
      an immutable graph
